title: Sprint 1 planning on observability and stability of the payment flow.

metadata.focus: Start of the series: foundational commitments for observability, support, and provider decision.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: In this sprint, I want to tackle the most painful part of the payment flow. We keep reacting too late when something goes wrong, and support has no way to explain what happened.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: Totally. When the customer says that the charge got stuck halfway, the only thing we can do is ask for screenshots and escalate to development.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: The first thing to stop going in blind is to add structured logs to the payment service with request_id, tenant, and provider result. Without that, we can't correlate anything.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: If we don't have request_id from end to end, then in QA we also can't reconstruct which exact call broke.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Okay, that's a clear commitment for the sprint. I want us to treat it as the foundation for everything else.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: Also, we need a very basic dashboard for 5xx errors from checkout. With volume per hour and breakdown by code, we could already detect if there's a weird pattern.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: And on my end, I want alerts when the provider's timeouts go up. When the user is left waiting, the frontend is the one that first bears the frustration.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: It would also help support to have the provider's error codes documented. Right now, every time a new one appears, we have to ask Mikel what it means.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: I can put that on a page of the runbook. It's not complex, it's just that no one has taken the time to write it down.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Alright, so we have logs, dashboard, alerts, and runbook. I also want to note a question that we've been dragging along.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: We've been saying for months that maybe we need a secondary payment provider so we don't depend so much on the current one. I'm not saying to open it up now, but to review if it makes sense.

segments.speaker: Jon
segments.text: Right now, I wouldn't include it as hard work for the sprint. Without observability or comparable numbers, it would be deciding based on feelings.

segments.speaker: Mikel
segments.text: I agree. First, we need data. If we then see weird spikes or poor availability, we can discuss whether it's worth looking for an alternative.

segments.speaker: Leire
segments.text: So, the review of the secondary provider is noted, but we won't get into migration or long comparisons yet.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Correct. We'll leave that review pending, and the explicit decision for this sprint is to maintain the current provider while we set up observability.

segments.speaker: Nora
segments.text: That works for me if we really close the runbook issue, because right now support is completely in the dark.

segments.speaker: Ane
segments.text: Let's wrap up the planning: structured logs, 5xx dashboard, timeout alerts, error runbook, and the review of the secondary provider as an open issue for later.

expected_items.title: Add structured logs to the payment service with request_id and tenant.
expected_items.summary: Allow correlation of calls, errors, and provider response in the payment flow.

expected_items.title: Create a dashboard for 5xx errors from the checkout.
expected_items.summary: Visualize volume by hour and breakdown by code to detect failure patterns.

expected_items.title: Alert when the payment provider's timeouts increase.
expected_items.summary: Trigger an operational signal when the provider worsens and the user is left waiting.

expected_items.title: Document the provider's error codes for support.
expected_items.summary: Create a runbook page so support can interpret errors without relying on development.

expected_items.title: Review whether it is advisable to work with a secondary payment provider.
expected_items.summary: It is raised as a later review, still without a commitment to migration or formal comparison.

expected_items.title: Keep the current provider during the sprint while setting up observability.
expected_items.summary: The team decides not to move providers until real data on stability and errors is available.
